anatomy.hemisphere_symmetry: symmetric — this is a midline sagittal view, so symmetry cannot be assessed.

tissue_findings.gray_matter: Appears normal in signal intensity and morphology.
tissue_findings.white_matter: Appears normal in signal intensity and morphology. There are some patchy areas of slightly increased signal in the deep white matter, particularly anteriorly, which could represent normal perivascular spaces or very early gliosis, but are not definitively abnormal.
tissue_findings.ventricles: The lateral ventricles are normal in size and configuration. The third ventricle is not well visualized in this slice.
tissue_findings.sulci_gyri: The sulci and gyri are well-defined and appear normal in depth.
tissue_findings.basal_ganglia: The caudate nucleus, putamen, and globus pallidus are visible and appear normal.
tissue_findings.thalamus: The thalamus is visible and appears normal.
tissue_findings.cerebellum: The cerebellum is visible and appears normal.
tissue_findings.brainstem: The midbrain, pons, and medulla are visible and appear normal.

pathology_flags.notes: No acute intracranial abnormalities are identified.

global_impression: This sagittal T1-weighted MRI demonstrates normal brain anatomy without evidence of acute intracranial pathology. The gray and white matter appear unremarkable. The ventricles are normal in size.
